reforms.description: Luxembourg has introduced more targeted income-based R&D tax incentives to encourage research and development activities among firms, ensuring that only entities bearing the risk of developments located in Luxembourg receive relief. These incentives are likely to benefit larger firms disproportionately.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to stimulate R&D activities among firms, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into effect and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant adjustment to existing tax incentives, enhancing their targeting.
reforms.source_quote: The scheme was amended in April 2018 to only provide relief for entities bearing the risk of the developments that are located in Luxembourg.

reforms.description: The new government has outlined its planned policy reforms for the 2018-2023 legislative period, including measures to enhance digitalisation and productivity growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to improve productivity through digitalisation, which is expected to enhance economic performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has actively begun implementing the outlined reforms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This represents a structural change in the government's approach to innovation and productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The new government has outlined its planned policy reforms for the 2018-2023 legislative period in the Coalition Agreement of November 2018.

reforms.description: Luxembourg's government adopted the 'Smart Specialisation Strategy' to diversify the economy, focusing on knowledge-intensive sectors and establishing a Space Agency to enhance innovation policy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to foster innovation in key sectors, which can lead to economic diversification and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been officially adopted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the government's approach to sectoral R&D and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The process is based on inputs from the 'Third Industrial Revolution', a large foresight exercise commissioned by the government in 2015.

reforms.description: Luxembourg's government is fostering a growing startup ecosystem through dedicated seed funds like the Digital Tech Fund and Fit4Start, aimed at supporting innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The support for startups is expected to enhance innovation and create new economic opportunities.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has actively supported the startup ecosystem through various initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to bolster the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Luxembourg indeed succeeded in establishing itself as an ICT hub in Europe.

reforms.description: Luxembourg's government has created more favourable immigration schemes to attract qualified ICT workers, optimizing Blue Card requirements and lowering wage thresholds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting skilled ICT workers is expected to enhance the talent pool and support innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The new immigration schemes have been put into effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant adjustment to immigration policies aimed at skilled workers.
reforms.source_quote: The government created more favourable immigration schemes for a range of relevant occupations by optimizing Blue Card requirements and lowering the associated wage thresholds.

reforms.description: The 'Digital Skills Bridge' pilot programme provides technical and financial assistance to firms undergoing digital disruption, aiming to upskill employees and transition workers at risk of automation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Upskilling employees is crucial for maintaining competitiveness in a rapidly changing digital landscape.
reforms.status_evidence: The pilot programme has been launched and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to address skills gaps in the workforce.
reforms.source_quote: The 'Digital Skills Bridge' aims to fill this gap by providing firms undergoing digital disruption with technical and financial assistance to upskill their employees.

reforms.description: Luxembourg's innovation agency, Luxinnovation, has expanded its role to support digital adoption in SMEs by linking them with external experts and providing tailored recommendations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting SMEs in digital adoption is expected to enhance their productivity and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The agency has actively begun supporting SMEs in their digital transformation efforts.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of Luxinnovation's role represents a significant adjustment in support for SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: Luxinnovation also supports digital adoption in SMEs by linking firms with external experts.

reforms.description: Luxembourg has launched a national AI strategy to enhance skills related to AI development and use, aiming to position public administration as a frontrunner in AI.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to build capabilities in AI, which is expected to drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This represents a significant commitment to advancing AI capabilities in the public sector.
reforms.source_quote: Luxembourg will shortly launch its national AI strategy.
